title: [lichen_l2.c:432-444] peer_find_oldest_locked returns -1 on empty table but caller checks slot < 0
description: peer_find_oldest_locked() returns -1 if no active peer is found. The caller at line 621 checks 'if (slot < 0)' which catches this. However, if peer_table has all entries with active=false, oldest_idx stays -1 and the function returns -1. But the caller then logs 'Peer table inconsistent: full but no eviction candidate'. This message is misleading - it's not inconsistent, the table is simply empty. The code path to line 620 only happens when slot < 0 after the empty-slot search (lines 605-610) fails, meaning all entries are active. But then peer_find_oldest_locked should find one. The error message is correct but confusing.
close_reason: Fixed: Improved error message to clarify this is a logic bug (loops disagree on active count), not table inconsistency
